context.game: Pico Echo - virtual pet game where player cares for Echo the cockatiel
context.role: Sleep state during Echo's rest period (12-hour sleep cycle managed by TimeSystem)
context.screen_position: Center at x=64px, y=60px on perch
adjacent_elements: zzz_particle (appears to right of Echo at x=80-90px, y=40-50px)
context.usage_scenario: Automatically triggered during sleep hours set by player. Echo cannot be interacted with during this state. ZZZ particles appear above/beside Echo. Transitions to idle when sleep period ends.

visual_specification.shape: Cockatiel with head tucked into back feathers. Body appears more rounded/compact than idle state. Head is turned backward and buried in wing/back area. Crest is flattened against head. Overall silhouette is more circular and compressed.
visual_specification.texture: Pure pixel art, hand-drawn. Softer, more rounded appearance than alert states.

animation.description: Frame 1: Head fully tucked, body still. Frame 2: Subtle breathing motion - body expands 1px vertically. Frame 3: Body contracts back to frame 1 size. Very slow, gentle breathing animation to show Echo is alive but asleep.

visual_specification.details: Eyes are not visible (hidden in feathers). Beak not visible. Cheek patch partially visible (2x2px instead of full 4x4px). Crest flattened to 1-2px height. Body width increases by 2-3px due to fluffed feathers. Feet remain visible gripping perch. Overall height reduced by 4-6px compared to idle.

relationships.shared_with: Same color palette as all Echo states
relationships.differences_from: Unlike idle: head not visible, eyes hidden, crest flattened, body more compact and rounded. Unlike other states: minimal animation (only breathing), no eye contact, no interaction possible.

explicit_negations: No closed eyelids showing (eyes completely hidden)
